activation_manager.deviation_reason: Operator requested hotfix copy of an active worktree and ad hoc MT execution with recovery state, not the full pre-launch ceremony.

refinement.operator_request: Create a hotfix copy of wtc-native-editors-v1 for CKC/PoseKit overhaul under Atelier, create WP-CKC-posekit-overhaul, update taskboard/build-order/traceability, and use one microtask per future requested change before implementation.
refinement.project_intent: CKC and PoseKit should become native Handshake features, written and wired in the Rust/native architecture where possible, with every feature interconnected so other modules can use the outputs.
refinement.starting_priority: Address non-Rust language surfaces first, then perform visual and behavioral overhaul against the original proven test app.
scope_edges: This WP owns work in ../wtc-ckc-posekit-overhaul only.
The copied source dirty state is baseline context, not automatically in-scope for CKC commits.
Each product edit gets its own MT contract before implementation.
assumptions: The active source worktree dirty state must be preserved in the hotfix copy so the operator can compare and recover.
The original test app is available or can be located before parity MTs begin.
CKC/PoseKit currently span Rust backend and non-Rust frontend/test-app surfaces.
non_goals: No merge-to-main or validator PASS claim.
No visual/behavioral overhaul before the non-Rust surface pass unless the operator changes priority.
microtask_plan: MT-001 should inventory CKC/PoseKit/Atelier non-Rust surfaces and classify keep, migrate, replace, or delete.
Subsequent MTs should migrate one coherent surface or behavior at a time.
Visual/behavioral MTs should cite original-app evidence before edits.
verification_plan: Confirm hotfix worktree branch, source HEAD, dirty state match, and .GOV junction.
Run governance sync/checks after WP pointer updates.
For product MTs, run the narrow Rust/UI/test commands tied to the touched files.
Use visual debugging once GUI behavior is changed.

identified_risks: Cross-WP baseline contamination from copied dirty files.
Original-app production value lost during native rewrite.
Premature deletion of non-Rust code before replacement is complete.
Parallel MT conflicts in native shell or Atelier files.
Recovery drift if MT index and packet are not updated after ad hoc changes.
minimum_controls: Path-limited staging and diff review per MT.
Original-app parity evidence before visual/behavioral changes.
MT target-file declarations before product edits.
mt_index.json active_mt_id updated during active implementation.
Packet and WP communications updated after each MT.
